learning_outcomes.description: Understand discussions on topics covered in the course and make sense of  spoken stories and anecdotes.
Participate in a discussion on topics covered in the course and express your opinion clearly, recount personal events and stories.
Understand simple texts which describe events and feelings; understand key points of French press articles.
Provide structured written arguments on topics covered in the course and recount a story.
Use a range of verb tenses and moods effectively.
Show awareness of Francophone cultural practices and points of view on familiar topics.
Pronounce French with minimal phonetic errors.

assessments.due_date:
31/07/2026 5:00 pm

To be completed by Friday, WEEK 1.

Students who are not able to meet the due date (due to late enrolment) must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to ensure they comply with this requirement.
assessments.task_description:
All students are required to demonstrate they have been correctly placed in FREN2020. This is a course requirement.  No assessment items will be marked until this requirement has been completed. If the placement result shows that you should not be enrolled in FREN2020, but in a higher course, your grade is capped at a 3.

The results of your placement will be recorded in Gradebook on the course website.

During the Course Placement questionnaire, you may be asked to write a short text in French. This must be your own unassisted work so we can accurately place you.

Statement on use of Generative AI and Machine Translation:

Completion of this Language Placement Questionnaire is a compliance hurdle requirement in many courses. This requirement aims to ensure that you are placed in the most appropriate language course based on your current level of linguistic proficiency. Your responses, along with the possible submission of short writing or speaking sample, help us match you to a course that aligns with your previous (formal or informal) language learning experiences. While this is an unsupervised, individual online task, it is not in your best interest to use generative AI tools or Machine Translation. If the work you submit does not reflect your actual language ability, you may be placed in a course that is not appropriate for your level, which could negatively affect your learning and performance. This may also be considered an act of student misconduct under the UQ Student Code of Conduct due to the misrepresentation of your capabilities.

Finally, please note that all language courses include at least one assessment task where the use of generative AI or MT tools is either not allowed or of limited value. These assessments are designed to be authentic, challenging, and based on real-time interaction. You will be expected to demonstrate a clear understanding of your own work without the assistance of AI or MT tools
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Since this is a pass/ fail item, there is no late submission penalty as such. However, no work will be marked by staff until placement in the course has been confirmed.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
31/08/2026 - 4/09/2026

Interviews will be scheduled in WEEK 6 outside class. The exact days and times will be released on the course website.
assessments.task_description:
In Week 4, students will be given a communicative task to prepare, based on material studied in class. On the day of the assessment, they will engage spontaneously with their teacher to discuss the topic they have prepared. The oral will last for 5-6 minutes.

This assessment item allows students to demonstrate competency at B1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Production, Spoken Interaction and Listening. (Link to Common European Frame of Reference). 

Format: Students will complete their oral in person, face to face.

Recording: The orals will be video-recorded so that there is a record of the performance. 

Statement on AI: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Oral interviews will be conducted in person.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete a short written exam in the second class of Week 7.

The questions will be a mixture of multiple choice, short answer and short paragraph, and will be based on material from Défi 2 and other materials studied in class.

Exam adjustments: If you have an agreed set of Alternative Exam Adjustments, you will need to apply for them, by the specified deadline, to be activated for this assessment item.

Statement on AI

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Weekly tutorials

Tutorial activities include discovering:

  • Francophone culture practices and points of view on topics studied; 
  • small group speaking tasks (focus on communication, accuracy and pronunciation);
  • listening for specific info (focus on comprehension and/or pronunciation); 
  • reading of short texts and a short novel, L'étranger for general comprehension and reading for specific information (vocabulary, structure, cultural information);
  • communicating in written French (focus on grammatical structures and vocabulary).

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_description: Weekly tutorials Tutorial activities include discovering: Francophone culture practices and points of view on topics studied; small group speaking tasks (focus on communication, accuracy and pronunciation); listening for specific info (focus on comprehension and/or pronunciation); reading of short texts and a short novel, L'étranger for general comprehension and reading for specific information (vocabulary, structure, cultural information); communicating in written French (focus on grammatical structures and vocabulary).
